meta.title: Custom AI vs ChatGPT for Business: Solution Comparison
meta.description: Structured comparison of building a custom AI solution versus using ChatGPT (or similar off-the-shelf LLM APIs) for enterprise business applications.

custom_ai.name: Custom AI Solution
custom_ai.description: Purpose-built AI system using fine-tuned or RAG-augmented models, proprietary data pipelines, and custom UX integrated into existing workflows

chatgpt_api.name: ChatGPT / Off-the-shelf LLM API
chatgpt_api.description: Direct integration with OpenAI ChatGPT, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, or similar third-party LLM APIs with prompt engineering

custom_ai.notes: Weeks to months for data pipeline, model selection, fine-tuning, and integration

chatgpt_api.notes: Hours to days; API key + prompt engineering + integration; quick proof of concept

custom_ai.notes: Fine-tuned on your data; understands proprietary terminology, processes, documents

chatgpt_api.notes: General knowledge; requires detailed system prompts; RAG improves but adds complexity

custom_ai.notes: Full control; on-premises or private cloud; no data leaves your environment

chatgpt_api.notes: Data sent to third-party servers; enterprise agreements help but data residency concerns remain

custom_ai.notes: Fully controllable; can meet any compliance requirement with proper implementation

chatgpt_api.notes: OpenAI/Azure offer BAAs and compliance certifications; still requires vendor trust

custom_ai.notes: High upfront; lower marginal cost at volume; fixed infrastructure vs per-token pricing

chatgpt_api.notes: Per-token pricing scales linearly; high-volume usage can cost $10,000 to $100,000+/mo

custom_ai.notes: $50,000 to $500,000+ for full custom build; GPU infrastructure; ML engineering team

chatgpt_api.notes: $0 to $5,000 to integrate; minimal engineering; pay only for usage

custom_ai.notes: Depends on base model and fine-tuning quality; smaller models may underperform

chatgpt_api.notes: Frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5) excel at general reasoning, writing, code

custom_ai.notes: Full control of model behavior, outputs, guardrails, and integration

chatgpt_api.notes: Limited to prompt engineering and fine-tuning; vendor controls model updates and policy

custom_ai.notes: No single-vendor lock-in; model can be swapped or updated independently

chatgpt_api.notes: API changes, pricing shifts, or deprecations require reactive engineering

custom_ai.notes: Ongoing model retraining, infrastructure management, data pipeline maintenance required

chatgpt_api.notes: Vendor handles model updates; engineering effort limited to prompt and integration changes

custom_ai.notes: Can be deeply embedded; direct database access, real-time data, custom APIs

chatgpt_api.notes: Function calling and tool use enable integration; slightly more latency and complexity

custom_ai.notes: Possible with interpretability tools; log and audit every inference internally

chatgpt_api.notes: Black-box vendor model; limited insight into why specific outputs were generated

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Proof of concept or pilot program
use_case_recommendations.reason: Fast to launch; validate business value before committing to custom build
Data sovereignty and compliance requirements make third-party APIs high-risk

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Customer support chatbot on general topics
use_case_recommendations.reason: Frontier models handle general queries well; RAG adds product-specific knowledge affordably

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Proprietary document analysis (legal, finance, engineering docs)
use_case_recommendations.reason: Fine-tuned model on domain corpus outperforms prompt-only approaches for specialized extraction
Per-token API costs at scale make self-hosted open-source models more economical

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Internal productivity tools for knowledge workers
use_case_recommendations.reason: Low volume, high quality needs; frontier model via API is fastest and highest quality path
